authors: U Mainz) is a professor of political communication at NTNU
U Mainz) is a professor of media sociology at NTNU
U Hohenheim–Stuttgart) is a professor of communication at JGU
raw_title: Do Journalists’ Political Orientations Translate into Partisan News Reporting? The Limits of Bias and the Limits of Counter Mechanisms
title_en: Do Journalists’ Political Orientations Translate into Partisan News Reporting? The Limits of Bias and the Limits of Counter Mechanisms
title_zh: 记者的政治倾向会转化为党派新闻报道吗？偏见的局限性与反制机制的局限性
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨记者个人的政治观点是否会影响他们的新闻报道，使其带有党派色彩。研究发现，即使记者有政治倾向，也不一定会直接导致偏颇的报道，因为新闻机构内部存在各种机制来防止这种情况发生。但研究也指出，这些反制机制本身也有局限性，不能完全消除偏见。
plain_summary_en: This study examines whether journalists' personal political views influence their news reporting to become partisan. The research finds that even when journalists have political leanings, it doesn't necessarily lead to biased reporting because news organizations have various mechanisms to prevent this. However, the study also notes that these counter-mechanisms themselves have limitations and cannot completely eliminate bias.
research_direction_zh: 探索新闻生产过程中个人因素与组织因素如何相互作用，研究新闻专业主义在限制政治偏见方面的实际效果，以及媒体机构内部质量控制机制的局限性。
research_direction_en: Investigating how individual factors and organizational factors interact in news production processes, examining the actual effectiveness of journalistic professionalism in limiting political bias, and exploring the limitations of quality control mechanisms within media organizations.
